# rc4

```go
import "crypto/rc4"
```

Package rc4 implements RC4 encryption, as defined in Bruce Schneier's Applied Cryptography.

RC4 is cryptographically broken and should not be used for secure applications.

<a name="Cipher"></a>

## type [Cipher](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/crypto/rc4/rc4.go#L20-L23>)

A Cipher is an instance of RC4 using a particular key.

```go
type Cipher struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="NewCipher"></a>

### func [NewCipher](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/crypto/rc4/rc4.go#L33>)

```go
func NewCipher(key []byte) (*Cipher, error)
```

NewCipher creates and returns a new [Cipher](<#Cipher>). The key argument should be the RC4 key, at least 1 byte and at most 256 bytes.

<a name="Cipher.Reset"></a>

### func \(\*Cipher\) [Reset](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/crypto/rc4/rc4.go#L57>)

```go
func (c *Cipher) Reset()
```

Reset zeros the key data and makes the [Cipher](<#Cipher>) unusable.

Deprecated: Reset can't guarantee that the key will be entirely removed from the process's memory.

<a name="Cipher.XORKeyStream"></a>

### func \(\*Cipher\) [XORKeyStream](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/crypto/rc4/rc4.go#L64>)

```go
func (c *Cipher) XORKeyStream(dst, src []byte)
```

XORKeyStream sets dst to the result of XORing src with the key stream. Dst and src must overlap entirely or not at all.
